user.title: Rain Bird IQ4
user.description: Choose how Home Assistant should connect to Rain Bird IQ4. Browser token is recommended when Rain Bird password login is blocked by AWS WAF.

password.title: Rain Bird IQ4 account
password.description: Sign in with your Rain Bird 2.0 / IQ4 account. This is not the 6 digit controller PIN. If Rain Bird asks for a browser challenge, the next screen will let you use a browser token instead.

browser_token.title: Rain Bird IQ4 browser token
browser_token.description:
1. Open the [Rain Bird token helper]({token_helper_url}).
2. Drag the helper bookmarklet to your bookmarks bar.
3. Use the helper to open Rain Bird login and sign in normally.
4. After the Rain Bird web app loads, click the bookmarklet. The helper will save the token to this Home Assistant setup session.
5. Return here and click Submit. You can leave the field below empty.

Fallback: paste a final Rain Bird `https://iq4.rainbird.com/auth.html#...` URL or a raw `access_token` below.

data.token_or_callback_url: Final Rain Bird URL or access token

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to Rain Bird IQ4.
error.invalid_auth: Rain Bird IQ4 rejected the username, password, or token.
error.invalid_token: Could not find an access token. Paste the full final Rain Bird URL or the raw access_token value.
error.token_missing: No token has been captured yet. Open the token helper, send the token from Rain Bird, then submit again.
error.token_expired: That access token is already expired. Open the Rain Bird login link again and paste the new final URL.
error.waf_challenge: Rain Bird IQ4 returned an AWS WAF browser challenge. Use browser token sign-in instead.

abort.already_configured: This Rain Bird IQ4 account is already configured.
abort.reauth_successful: Rain Bird IQ4 browser token was updated.
